channel.title: Hardcore History Archives
channel.description: Private backup feed for the entire collection of Dan Carlin's Hardcore History episodes.

item.title: Alexander Versus Hitler
item.description: Dan compares the way the modern world sees Adolf Hitler with the way history views Alexander the Great and wonders if the two men were not more alike than different.

item.title: Guns and Horses
item.description: The West is Mike Tyson and the East is Muhammad Ali in this episode as Dan looks at a possible reason for the military decline of the near East.

item.title: The Organization of Peace
item.description: The early 20th century League of Nations stood for peace, cooperation, disarmament and international understanding. What happened?

item.title: Romancing the Tribes
item.description: Shawnee chief Tecumseh and Arverni king Vercingetorix both tried to unify their tribal peoples to face overwhelming foes. Dan examines the romance of their lost causes.

item.title: Meandering Through The Cold War
item.description: Dan meanders through a conversation about causes and effects of the Cold War without ever talking much about the actual events of the conflict.

item.title: Shield of the West
item.description: Spartans, Athenians, Persians and references to Star Trek all make appearances in this look at the dramatic and extremely consequential Greek and Persian wars.

item.title: The X-History Files
item.description: Napoleon is supposed to have said that “History is a set of lies agreed upon”. With that in mind, Dan looks at some of the alternative and pseudo history ideas that many people embrace.

item.title: Scars of the Great War
item.description: Dan looks at the shock and awe caused by the First World War and marvels at how connected we still are to the empire-shattering effects of the conflict.

item.title: Darkness Buries the Bronze Age
item.description: What was the cause of the collapse of the Bronze Age? War? Famine? Natural disaster? Dan looks at all the potential villains.

item.title: The What-Ifs of 1066
item.description: How might history have been changed if the events of the momentous year 1066 had turned out differently? Dan examines Normans, Vikings, and Saxons.

item.title: Thoughts on Churchill
item.description: Dan takes a look at various elements of the dramatic life and career of the great British leader Winston Churchill.

item.title: Steppe Stories
item.description: Blood-sucking Scythian warriors, tattooed ice mummies, Amazons killing so they can mate, head-hunters, and scalp-taking nomads of the Eurasian steppe.

item.title: Bubonic Nukes
item.description: What would happen if half the population died in a short period of time? Dan looks at the Black Death and other plagues that created apocalyptic conditions.

item.title: The Macedonian Soap Opera
item.description: When Alexander the Great bequeathed his empire "to the strongest" he set off a funeral contest that shook the world for decades. Murder, marriage, intrigue, and drama.

item.title: Desperate Times
item.description: The tough economic climate after the First World War fostered the growth of radicalism. Did it also create tougher people? Dan looks at the "Greatest Generation".

item.title: Nazi Tidbits
item.description: What accounts for the fascination people still have with Adolf Hitler and the intensity of the German National Socialists? Dan examines the charismatic Nazi Führer.

item.title: Judgment at Nineveh
item.description: Will our modern society ever decline and fall? Dan uses that idea as a backdrop for a look at the first great empire in history, the biblical-era Assyrians.

item.title: A Fly on James Burke's Wall
item.description: Dan sits down for an extended and fascinating interview with legendary historian, writer, and "Connections" television host James Burke.

item.title: Apache Tears
item.description: A deep, empathetic, and brutal look at the clash of cultures between Native American Apache tribes and the encroaching forces of the United States military.

item.title: (Blitz) History Under the Influence
item.description: Dan looks at how altered states of consciousness, alcohol, and drugs have influenced major historical figures and key turning points in human history.

item.title: Punic Nightmares I
item.description: Part one of Dan's epic trilogy on the titanic, zero-sum struggle between the Roman Republic and the empire of Carthage for control of the Mediterranean.

item.title: Punic Nightmares II
item.description: Part two of the Punic Wars saga focuses on Hannibal Barca's legendary tactical genius and his devastating invasion of the Italian heartland.

item.title: Punic Nightmares III
item.description: The final chapter of the Punic Wars, culminating in Scipio Africanus's counter-strategy and the total, apocalyptic destruction of the city of Carthage.

item.title: Classical Hanson
item.description: Dan interviews military historian and classicist Victor Davis Hanson about ancient warfare, Greek citizen-soldiers, and lessons for the modern world.

item.title: The Dyer Outlook
item.description: An in-depth interview with journalist, historian, and geopolitical analyst Gwynne Dyer exploring contemporary global conflicts through a historical lens.

item.title: (Blitz) Addicted to Bondage
item.description: A sobering, conceptual look at the historical omnipresence of human slavery across ancient cultures and how human societies became dependent on it.

item.title: Ghosts of the Ostfront I
item.description: Part one of Dan's legendary four-part masterpiece on the Eastern Front of WWII, focusing on the psychological and ideological clash between Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union.

item.title: Ghosts of the Ostfront II
item.description: Part two follows the German war machine deeper into Soviet territory as winter sets in and the struggle turns into an industrialized meat-grinder.

item.title: Ghosts of the Ostfront III
item.description: Part three highlights the turning point of the entire European theater: the brutal urban warfare and encirclement at the Battle of Stalingrad.

item.title: Ghosts of the Ostfront IV
item.description: The harrowing conclusion to the Eastern Front series, charting the relentless Soviet counter-offensive and the apocalyptic fall of Berlin.

item.title: (Blitz) Suffer the Children
item.description: A deeply disturbing look at child-rearing practices, child abuse, and the societal trauma experienced by children throughout different eras of human history.

item.title: Globalization Unto Death
item.description: The epic and terrifying story of Ferdinand Magellan’s armada and the first, incredibly deadly circumnavigation of the globe.

item.title: (Blitz) Old School Toughness
item.description: Dan tackles a perennial human question: Were previous generations of human beings physically and mentally tougher than we are today?

item.title: Death Throes of the Republic I
item.description: Part one of Dan's massive six-part epic tracking the multi-generational political decay, civil wars, and systemic collapse of the Roman Republic.

item.title: Death Throes of the Republic II
item.description: Part two explores the populist reforms of the Gracchi brothers and how political violence became normalized in the Roman senate.

item.title: Death Throes of the Republic III
item.description: Part three highlights the military reforms of Marius and the terrifying rise of Rome's first true warlord-dictator, Lucius Cornelius Sulla.

item.title: Death Throes of the Republic IV
item.description: Part four charts the rise of the next generation of ambitious Romans, focusing heavily on Pompey the Great and the Gladiator revolt led by Spartacus.

item.title: Death Throes of the Republic V
item.description: Part five focuses squarely on Julius Caesar, his brilliant military campaigns in Gaul, and the political corner he was backed into by his enemies.

item.title: Death Throes of the Republic VI
item.description: The colossal, 5.5-hour conclusion. Caesar crosses the Rubicon, a global civil war erupts, and the Roman Republic is definitively buried under the foundations of the Empire.

item.title: (Blitz) Radical Thoughts
item.description: Using the first and second American "Red Scare" eras as case studies, Dan analyzes what happens to a free society when it is paralyzed by ideological paranoia.

item.title: Thor's Angels
item.description: A sweeping, 4-hour audio-monolith tracking the collapse of the Western Roman Empire and the rise of the brutal, sword-wielding Germanic tribes that reshaped early medieval Europe.

item.title: (Blitz) Logical Insanity
item.description: An intense exploration of the moral and strategic calculations behind the transition to total aerial warfare in WWII, culminating in the atomic bombings of Japan.

item.title: Wrath of the Khans I
item.description: Part one of Dan's legendary Mongol series. A little-known tribe of Eurasian horse nomads unites under Genghis Khan and begins a violent explosion across the globe.

item.title: Wrath of the Khans II
item.description: Part two analyzes the terrifying, highly advanced military machine built by Genghis Khan and his brutal campaigns against the wealthy empires of China and Persia.

item.title: Wrath of the Khans III
item.description: Part three charts the unstoppable westward expansion of the Mongol hordes, pushing deep into central Asia and the Middle East with terrifying psychological warfare.

item.title: Wrath of the Khans IV
item.description: The death of Genghis Khan does nothing to slow down the conquest. Part four follows his successors as they turn their sights toward the kingdoms of Europe.

item.title: Wrath of the Khans V
item.description: The epic conclusion to the Mongol saga. Internicene succession issues arise among Genghis's grandchildren, creating fracturing lines across the massive empire.

item.title: Prophets of Doom
item.description: A wild, 4.5-hour narrative charting the Münster Rebellion of 1534, where a radical group of anabaptist preachers took over a German city with apocalyptic results.

item.title: The American Peril
item.description: Dan examines the Spanish-American War, exploring the exact moment the United States wrestled with imperial temptation and global expansionism.

item.title: Blueprint for Armageddon I
item.description: Part one of Dan's monumental WWI series. The long nineteenth century fractures as a combination of alliance webs, nationalism, and an assassination ignites a global catastrophe.

item.title: Blueprint for Armageddon II
item.description: Part two looks at the opening rounds of the war, the horrific reality of modern industrialized fire, and the rapid failure of the initial plans of maneuver.

item.title: Blueprint for Armageddon III
item.description: Part three highlights the solidification of the Western Front trench systems and the psychological toll of a war that refused to end quickly.

item.title: Blueprint for Armageddon IV
item.description: Machine guns, barbed wire, mud, and millions of artillery shells create a literal hell on earth. Part four focuses on the attrition warfare of 1916.

item.title: Blueprint for Armageddon V
item.description: Domestic strain, political revolution, and deep military mutinies take center stage as the nations of Europe begin pushing past their breaking points.

item.title: Blueprint for Armageddon VI
item.description: The colossal finale to the WWI series. German Spring Offensives crash against Allied defenses, the Americans arrive in force, and a shattered world finally falls silent.

item.title: Kings of Kings I
item.description: Part one of Dan's trilogy on the Achaemenid Persian Empire, reframing their culture away from Greek propaganda and exploring the genius of Cyrus the Great.

item.title: Kings of Kings II
item.description: Part two moves from biblical-era coup conspiracies to the epic administrative expansion under Darius and the opening conflicts with the Greek city-states.

item.title: Kings of Kings III
item.description: The cinematic conclusion to the Persian trilogy, tracing the massive invasion by Xerxes and the eventual, world-altering conquests of Alexander the Great.

item.title: (Blitz) The Destroyer of Worlds
item.description: A massive, 6-hour look at the terrifying dawn of the atomic age, charting the Cold War nuclear standoff from Hiroshima through the Cuban Missile Crisis.

item.title: The Celtic Holocaust
item.description: A sweeping, brutal 6-hour epic detailing Julius Caesar’s systematic, genocidal conquest of tribal Gaul and the ferocious resistance led by Vercingetorix.

item.title: (Blitz) Painfotainment
item.description: A dark, philosophical 4.5-hour deep dive into the history of public torture and executions as a form of mass public entertainment across human history.

item.title: Supernova in the East I
item.description: Part one of Dan's 6-part masterpiece on the Asia-Pacific War, looking at the unique, deeply imperialistic, and ultra-nationalist internal culture of early 20th-century Japan.

item.title: Supernova in the East II
item.description: Part two looks at the brutal escalation of Japanese military interventions in China and the final geopolitical chess moves leading up to December 7, 1941.

item.title: Supernova in the East III
item.description: The Japanese empire explodes across the Pacific theater, creating a lightning-fast expansion and introducing a savage vibe to the warfare.

item.title: Supernova in the East IV
item.description: Part four maps out three of the most critical, iconic naval battles of the Pacific war: Coral Sea, Midway, and the meatgrinder of Guadalcanal.

item.title: Supernova in the East V
item.description: As industrial, numeric, and material resource superiorities turn decisively toward the Americans, the Japanese military tests the limits of suicidal devotion.

item.title: Supernova in the East VI
item.description: The harrowing, 5.5-hour conclusion to the Pacific war saga, detailing firebombing campaigns, kamikaze normization, and the atomic closing act.

item.title: (Blitz) Human Resources
item.description: A massive, visceral 5.5-hour deep dive exploring the horrific historical, commercial, and human dynamics of the Atlantic Slave Trade.

item.title: Twilight of the Aesir I
item.description: Picking up where Thor's Angels left off, Dan follows the violent, early medieval world of the pagan Viking Sea Kings as their culture collides with a changing Europe.

item.title: Twilight of the Aesir II
item.description: A massive 6.5-hour look at the late Viking age, charting characters like Harald Bluetooth and Svein Forkbeard as they transition into Christian monarchs.

item.title: Mania for Subjugation I
item.description: Part one of Dan's series exploring Alexander the Great, tracing his origin story via the recipe of Nature, Nurture, and Macedonian Nepotism.

item.title: Mania for Subjugation II
item.description: Part two finds a young 20-year-old Alexander navigating political assassination, consolidating his brutal grip on power, and fighting off threats in all directions.

item.title: Mania for Subjugation III
item.description: At just 21 years old, Alexander launches his massive, gladiatorial campaign to invade the heart of the Persian Empire and secure his immortality.

item.title: (Extra) Thor’s Angels
item.description: A bonus follow-up episode where Dan delves into extra material, listener feedback, and further thoughts on the Germanic tribes and the Dark Ages.

item.title: (Extra) Logical Insanity
item.description: Dan returns to the intense debate surrounding the morality of total war, discussing the responses to his Logical Insanity episode and expanding on the historical context.

item.title: (Extra) Wrath of the Khans
item.description: A bonus show released shortly after the finale, containing extra military history context and deeper cuts that didn't make it into the main narrative.
